summary: Tranxamic acid serves more general purposes rather than addressing underlying clotting deficiencies seen in haemophiliacs; common treatment involves replacing deficient blood-clotting proteins such as Factors VIII or IX which are absent here. Thus, \boxed{D}. The most appropriate choice based on typical therapeutic approaches remains D.
